compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: ROOT-CAUSE (confirm/deny of the cached-Pass-1 hypothesis): DENIED as causal. Tracing the path — plan_review_verify_inputs (workflow_ops.py:278) builds instructions as [verify_instructions(c) for c in (chunks or [[]])], so the PRODUCER always emits >=1 element even when every finding is omitted; and prompt caching (runs.py resolve_prompt_cached) does not touch the findings/instructions payload, so a fully-cache-served Pass-1 cannot by itself empty the instructions list. The ~21ms/llm_calls=26 the reporter saw is a LATENCY correlation of a cache-hit finder batch, not the cause. The actual defect is the CONSUMER contract: RunnerAgentStep.run treated an empty chunks list as 'run the model zero times' and _merge_chunked_outputs([]) returned {} as a clean success, so verify_requests=0 and every finding degraded to no-verification with empty stderr — indistinguishable from a healthy review. FIX (landed via change 304, bed48d6d8): the consumer now logs a diagnostic and dispatches one aggregate call on an empty instructions list (never silent), with a regression test. The precise transient by which instructions reached [] in the single reported f93a run was not reproducible offline, but the guard makes a silent zero-dispatch impossible regardless of origin.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Consumer-side silent zero-dispatch guard + diagnostic + regression test landed and CI-green. Cached-Pass-1 trigger hypothesis analyzed and DENIED as causal (see root-cause comment); the exact transient origin of the empty instructions list in the single reported run was not reproducible offline, so the AC asking for a live cached-Pass-1 path test cannot be satisfied. Closing uncertified by design; the fix prevents the silent-INDETERMINATE harm regardless of origin.". Session: 2bae0ea3d.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Run `rebar review-plan f93a-b947-558b-469c --output json` (a story with a ~2.6KB plan).
2. Observe the returned coverage.metrics + verdict.

Observed across 3 consecutive runs (attempts 3/4/5 in this session; cache cleared before attempt 5):

Expected: Pass-2 verify dispatches over the Pass-1 findings (verify_requests > 0), findings are
verified/decided, verdict is PASS or BLOCK. (This is what attempt 1 on an earlier revision of the same
ticket did: verify_requests=43, llm_ms=553581, verdict BLOCK with a verified finding.)

Actual: verdict INDETERMINATE with verify_requests=0, llm_ms~=21ms despite llm_calls=26, total_ms~=16s,
0x HTTP 429, and a COMPLETELY EMPTY stderr (no warning/error/exception logged). All 17 Pass-1 findings
fall through to `indeterminate` with reason `no-verification` because Pass-2 never dispatched. The result
is deterministically reproducible on this ticket's current plan; clearing `.rebar/cache` (which had no
entry for this ticket anyway) did not change it.

## Why it matters
A silent Pass-2 non-dispatch means the gate can return INDETERMINATE (fail-closed / non-signing) for a
plan that has no blocking issue, with NO diagnostic in stderr — indistinguishable to an operator from a
healthy review. It also blocks earning a fresh attestation after a material edit. The llm_ms~=21ms /
llm_calls=26 mismatch suggests Pass-1 was served from a cache (Anthropic prompt cache or other) and a
fully-cached Pass-1 may be the trigger for Pass-2 being skipped — needs root-causing.

## Context
Surfaced while re-reviewing story f93a (eval-foundation, epic sure-foyer-aroma ac1b) after remediating a
legitimate BLOCK. Evidence JSONs: ~/.claude/jobs/19885ca3/tmp/planrev/f93a_rereview{,3,4,5}.json
(attempt 1 = healthy verify; 3/4/5 = degraded). rebar 0.5.2 (c12ad713b-dirty).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root-cause why Pass-2 verify reports verify_requests=0 (llm_ms~=21ms, llm_calls=26) reproducibly on
      this plan while attempt 1 verified normally; confirm/deny the cached-Pass-1 trigger hypothesis.
- [ ] A degraded/skipped verify pass emits a diagnostic (stderr log + a coverage note) — never silent.
- [ ] Either the gate re-dispatches verify on a cached Pass-1, or documents+justifies skipping it.
- [ ] A regression test covers the cached-Pass-1 -> verify path so this cannot silently recur.
compiled_state.title: Plan-review Pass-2 verify silently non-dispatches (verify_requests=0) reproducibly, yielding INDETERMINATE with empty stderr
